title: Translation From The Gull Language.
content:
В В В В В В В В В В В В Scripta manet.


В В В В 'Twas graved on the Stone of Destiny,[1]
В В В В In letters four and letters three;
В В В В And ne'er did the King of the Gulls go by
В В В В But those awful letters scared his eye;
В В В В For he knew that a Prophet Voice had said,
В В В В "As long as those words by man were read,
В В В В "The ancient race of the Gulls should ne'er
В В В В "One hour of peace or plenty share."
В В В В But years on years successive flew,
В В В В And the letters still more legible grew,--
В В В В At top, a T, an H, an E,
В В В В And underneath, D. E. B. T.

В В В В Some thought them Hebrew,--such as Jews
В В В В More skilled in Scrip than Scripture use;
В В В В While some surmised 'twas an ancient way
В В В В Of keeping accounts, (well known in the day
В В В В Of the famed Didlerius Jeremias,
В В В В Who had thereto a wonderful bias,)
В В В В And proved in books most learnedly boring,
В В В В 'Twas called the Pontick way of scoring.

В В В В Howe'er this be there never were yet
В В В В Seven letters of the alphabet,
В В В В That 'twixt them formed so grim a spell,
В В В В Or scared a Land of Gulls so well,
В В В В As did this awful riddle-me-ree
В В В В Of T. H. E. D. E. B. T.

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В Hark!--it is struggling Freedom's cry;
В В В В "Help, help, ye nations, or I die;
В В В В "'Tis Freedom's fight and on the field
В В В В "Where I expire your doom is sealed."
В В В В The Gull-King hears the awakening call,
В В В В He hath summoned his Peers and Patriots all,
В В В В And he asks. "Ye noble Gulls, shall we
В В В В "Stand basely by at the fall of the Free,
В В В В "Nor utter a curse nor deal a blow?"
В В В В And they answer with voice of thunder, "No."

В В В В Out fly their flashing swords in the air!--
В В В В But,--why do they rest suspended there?
В В В В What sudden blight, what baleful charm,
В В В В Hath chilled each eye and checkt each arm?
В В В В Alas! some withering hand hath thrown
В В В В The veil from off that fatal stone,
В В В В And pointing now with sapless finger,
В В В В Showeth where dark those letters linger,--
В В В В Letters four and letters three,
В В В В T. H. E. D. E. B. T.

В В В В At sight thereof, each lifted brand
В В В В Powerless falls from every hand;
В В В В In vain the Patriot knits his brow,--
В В В В Even talk, his staple, fails him now.
В В В В In vain the King like a hero treads,
В В В В His Lords of the Treasury shake their heads;
В В В В And to all his talk of "brave and free,"
В В В В No answer getteth His Majesty
В В В В But "T. H. E. D. E. B. T."

В В В В In short, the whole Gull nation feels
В В В В They're fairly spell-bound, neck and heels;
В В В В And so, in the face of the laughing world,
В В В В Must e'en sit down with banners furled,
В В В В Adjourning all their dreams sublime
В В В В Of glory and war to-some other time.
meaning:
"Translation From The Gull Language." is a quintessential example of Thomas Moore's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
